title: 10 best herbal teas for chills

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with chills because of its potent antiviral and antibacterial properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea purpurea, has been traditionally used to combat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections that often cause chills. When consumed in tea form, the active compounds in purple coneflower work to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system, helping to alleviate symptoms such as shivering and fever. As a result, drinking purple coneflower tea may provide relief from chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Purple coneflower tea contains compounds like triterpenoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause illness and contribute to chills.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and saponins present in purple coneflower tea may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as fever, headache, and body aches.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate symptoms of illness like chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which help to combat chills by stimulating immune response.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems, contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating chill symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning for optimal potency.
Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea or two teaspoons of fresh echinacea purpurea per 8 oz cup of boiling water.
Steep the echinacea mixture in hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and leaves.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and drink immediately to help alleviate chills and promote recovery.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with chills because it contains a unique blend of flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and regulate body temperature. The tea's warming properties help to increase blood circulation, allowing the body to better combat infection-causing pathogens and alleviate symptoms such as shivering and cold sweats. Additionally, maidenhair tree teas has natural antispasmodic and antiviral properties that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, further alleviating chills and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba tea contains antioxidants that can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to the sensation of chilliness.
Vasoactive compounds: The tea contains vasodilators like ginkgolides and bilobalide, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, potentially helping to warm up the body.
Antipyretic properties: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba has antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties, which may help alleviate the sensation of chilliness and fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, roots have been traditionally used to treat fever and cold symptoms, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal teas for chills.
Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains compounds like salicin, which is similar to aspirin and has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate chill-induced discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure 250-500mg of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per cup of tea.
Combine measured ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow for maximum extraction of herbal properties.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup.
Allow the tea to cool before consumption and serve warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with chills because of its natural antiviral and antibacterial properties, which soothe and calm the body's response to infection. The tea's flavonoids and antioxidants work together to reduce inflammation and promote a sense of warmth, helping to dispel shivers and shake off fatigue. Additionally, elder tea's calming effects can help ease anxiety and stress, common causes of chills, allowing the body to naturally recover from illness.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elder tea contains antiviral compounds that help combat viruses that cause colds and flu, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms like chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in elder tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce body aches, fever, and other uncomfortable symptoms associated with chills.

Expectorant properties: Elder tea has been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues like congestion, coughs, and colds, by helping to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs and airways, alleviating discomfort caused by chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce fever and alleviate cold-related symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe aching muscles and joints caused by chills.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves for every cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured sambucus nigra mixture with 200ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Allow the herbal tea to steep for 5-7 minutes to extract the active compounds and essential oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Serve the warm sambucus nigra tea immediately, sweetening with honey if desired, and drink as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with chills because of its natural warming properties. The menthol content in peppermint tea stimulates the body's thermoregulation, helping to increase blood flow and circulation, which can help to warm up the body from the inside out. Additionally, peppermint tea's soothing and calming effects can also help to reduce feelings of shivering and discomfort associated with chills. As a result, sipping on a warm cup of herbal peppermint tea can provide relief and comfort when feeling chilled to the bone.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: A natural decongestant that helps to ease congestion and relieve coughs, which can be a common symptom of chills.
Menthol: A natural analgesic that provides rapid relief from headaches, fever, and body aches associated with chills, by numbing the skin and mucous membranes.

Volatile oils: Containing menthone and isomenthone, these oils help to calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and stomach cramps that often occur when someone has chills, which can be a result of gastrointestinal upset or flu-like symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain high levels of menthol, which helps to cool and soothe the body, reducing symptoms of chills.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain a compound called tannin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce fever and ease cold-like symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh peppermint leaves or two teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves for brewing.
Rinse the fresh mint leaves or check the dried leaves for any debris or stems and remove them.
Heat one cup of water in a medium-sized pot over moderate heat until it reaches 212°f.
Steep the mint leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal release of essential oils.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool for at least 10-15 minutes before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with chills because of its natural calming properties, which can help to soothe the body and mind. The antiviral and antibacterial compounds present in lavender tea work together to reduce inflammation and promote a sense of warmth, alleviating chills and shivering. remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lavender tea's antioxidants help to combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of chills and other cold-like symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tea can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion, fever, and body aches associated with chills.
Anxiolytic properties: Lavender's calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for chills, and promote relaxation and restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant have been traditionally used to treat fever and chills due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Lavender leaves contain essential oils that help to reduce body temperature, ease pain, and promote sweating, making them effective in relieving chill symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers or use dried ones for every 8 oz of water.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried lavender flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the herbs to infuse and release their properties.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids before serving immediately.
Drink the tea slowly and calmly, savoring its soothing and calming effects for maximum benefit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which can help to increase circulation and bring warmth to the body. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce body aches, joint pain, and fever, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating chill symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginger root into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat one cup of boiling water and pour it over the ginger root in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor is reached.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Serve the warm tea immediately, with optional honey or lemon to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with chills because of its natural ability to stimulate circulation and reduce fever. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the body, reducing feelings of shivering and discomfort. remedy_properties: Antipyretic Properties: Yarrow tea has been shown to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, making it an effective natural remedy for treating colds and flu. This property helps to cool down the body and reduce inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The bioactive compounds present in yarrow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe sore throats, ease congestion, and reduce coughing fits that often accompany chills.

Antiviral and Antimicrobial Properties: Yarrow tea contains antiviral and antimicrobial agents that can help to combat the underlying infections causing chills, such as the common cold and flu viruses. This property helps to boost the immune system and fight off infection.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried achillea millefolium leaves and stems in a clean container for steeping.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried herb per eight ounces of boiling water and add it.
Pour the boiling water over the herb mixture and let it steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately after preparation, taking two cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with chills because of its unique blend of natural ingredients that work synergistically to soothe and calm the body. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves or purchase dried ones from a reputable source.

Barks: The barks of thyme contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to calm the body's response to infection and reduce inflammation, making them ideal for soothing chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves measuring one teaspoon for every 8 ounces of water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with chills because of its natural properties that promote warmth and comfort. remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in a clean glass container for further processing.
Boil one cup of water and let it simmer for five minutes before adding the herb.
Add two teaspoons of the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves to the boiling water and steep.
Allow the tea to steep for seven to ten minutes before straining and serving.

Drinking red clover tea may provide relief from the uncomfortable sensations caused by chills, allowing you to feel more comfortable and relaxed.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Red clover contains antiviral compounds that can help combat viral infections that cause chills, reducing their severity and duration.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate body aches, fever, and discomfort associated with chills, promoting a sense of well-being and relaxation.

Vasodilatory effects: Red clover's vasodilatory compounds can help widen blood vessels, improving circulation and increasing the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the body's cells, which can help combat chill-induced symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Red clover roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and body aches associated with chills.
Flowers: The flowers of red clover contain isoflavones, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue that come with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried red clover flowers according to local regulations and guidelines.
Measure one teaspoon of dried flowers or three teaspoons of fresh flowers per eight ounce cup water.
Steep the flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release beneficial compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and particles.
Drink the warm tea immediately, allowing it to cool slightly before consuming while experiencing chills.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with chills because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the body. The mucilage in marshmallow root coats the throat and chest, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to relax muscles and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a sense of warmth and comfort. As the body fights off an infection or illness, marshmallow teas provides gentle support and relief from the chills that often accompany it.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Marshmallow tea helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion associated with chills.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The mucilage in marshmallow root reduces inflammation and soothes irritated tissues, helping to alleviate discomfort and pain caused by chills.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Marshmallow tea has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the development of chills, reducing their severity and duration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated respiratory tracts, making it effective against chills.
Stems: Containing a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried marshmallow root and 1 cup boiling water.
Steep the marshmallow root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink immediately.
Prepare a fresh batch every 1-2 hours as needed throughout the day.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chills with herbal teas is to choose soothing options that promote relaxation and warmth. Popular choices include peppermint, chamomile, and lemon balm teas. These herbs have natural calming properties that help reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of chills. Steep a cup according to the package instructions and drink it slowly, inhaling the steam to further relax the body.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal teas are fever, colds, and influenza. Herbal teas such as peppermint, ginger, and eucalyptus can help alleviate symptoms of these ailments by warming the body and easing congestion. Other herbs like yarrow, elderflower, and willow bark can also be used to treat similar symptoms, promoting sweating and reducing inflammation to help bring down fever and relieve discomfort.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for chills are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the discomfort of cold temperatures. These teas typically contain warming spices and herbs such as ginger, cinnamon, and cayenne pepper, which work to increase blood flow and metabolism, making the body feel warmer. Examples of herbal teas for chills include ginger tea, cinnamon tea, and peppermint tea. By drinking these teas, individuals can find relief from chills, improve their overall circulation, and enhance their daily lives.
